doc_help_center_description: Learn how the Inner Anchor clinic system works, including treatments, payments, pickups, and subscriptions.
doc_overview_intro: Inner Anchor provides a comprehensive system for managing PRP treatments, from blood draw to patient pickup. This guide explains how each component works together.
doc_concept_treatment_desc: A PRP vial created from a patient's blood draw. Each treatment has a status, type, and payment status.
doc_concept_pickup_desc: A scheduled appointment for a patient to collect their treatment from the clinic.
doc_concept_credit_desc: A payment unit that allows a patient to schedule one pickup. Credits come from subscriptions or one-time payments.
doc_concept_subscription_desc: A monthly payment plan that automatically provides credits for regular pickups.
doc_treatment_intro: Treatments are created from blood draw appointments and progress through several statuses before being picked up by patients.
doc_type_prp_eye_drop: PRP Eye Drop - For dry eye treatment
doc_creating_treatments_desc: Treatments can be created from blood draw appointments or as ad-hoc entries from existing inventory.
doc_create_step_1: Schedule a blood draw appointment for the patient
doc_create_step_2: After the blood draw, create treatments from the appointment details page
doc_create_step_3: Specify the number of vials created and treatment type
doc_create_step_4: Generate labels for each vial with patient information and expiry date
doc_payment_intro: The credit system tracks patient payments and ensures pickups are properly paid for before scheduling.
doc_payment_pending_desc: Payment has not been received. The patient needs to pay before or during pickup.
doc_payment_paid_desc: One-time payment has been completed for this treatment.
doc_payment_subscription_desc: Payment is covered by the patient's active subscription.
doc_how_credits_work_desc: Credits are consumed when a patient schedules a pickup. The system automatically determines payment status based on available credits.
doc_credit_rule_1: Each paid subscription invoice grants 1 credit
doc_credit_rule_2: Each completed one-time payment grants 1 credit
doc_credit_rule_4: If no credits are available, the pickup is marked as pending payment
doc_no_credits_warning_desc: When a patient or clinic schedules a pickup or applies a treatment without available credits, a warning is displayed but the action can proceed.
doc_no_credits_rule_1: Patients scheduling a pickup with no credits will see a warning but can proceed anyway
doc_no_credits_rule_2: Clinics marking a treatment as administered with no patient credits will see a warning
doc_no_credits_rule_3: Available credits can go negative when treatments are applied without payment
doc_no_credits_rule_4: Negative credit balances indicate treatments that need payment arranged separately
doc_pickup_intro: Patients can schedule pickups through the patient app, selecting available time slots at their clinic.
doc_pickup_scheduled_desc: The pickup is booked and awaiting the patient.
doc_pickup_completed_desc: The patient has picked up their treatment.
doc_pickup_cancelled_desc: The pickup was cancelled by the patient or provider.
doc_provider_action_1: View scheduled pickups on the dashboard or patient appointments page
doc_provider_action_2: Mark pickups as completed when the patient arrives
doc_provider_action_3: Update payment status if payment was received separately
doc_provider_action_4: Add notes to pickups for special instructions or comments
doc_subscription_intro: Subscriptions provide a convenient way for patients to receive regular treatments with automatic monthly billing.
doc_benefit_2: No need to process payment at each pickup
doc_benefit_4: Easy subscription management through the patient app
doc_subscription_cancellation_desc: Patients can cancel their subscription through the patient app, or providers can cancel it from the patient's profile. After cancellation, patients retain access to any stored vials for 3 months.
expiration_date_for_treatments: The date when these treatments will expire
